prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a static graphic that does not yet show a car. On a dark background, white text reads “Prince Superoni / Prince Ave. / Lowell, Mass. / 01853.” A small portrait of a man appears in a rectangular inset at upper right, and a dark product package labeled “SUPERONI” is shown across the lower portion of the frame.

The scene then cuts to an outdoor car setting. A light-colored hatchback or wagon is parked with its rear liftgate open. An adult person stands near the open cargo area. Large translucent block letters spelling “NISSAN” are superimposed across the center of the image. Trees and daylight are visible in the background.

The following shots use heavy dissolves and overlapping images, making some details unclear. A light-colored car is shown from the side under leafy trees, with reflections or double exposure effects over the body. The camera then appears to move to the front/driver area of a car, with the windshield and dashboard area visible through layered imagery. A person inside or near the car is partially visible, but the overlapping frames make their exact position unclear.

The commercial shifts to driving imagery. A red car is seen on a road near a coastline or body of water, with hills or greenery in the background. The road and scenery are sunlit. This is followed by a close-up of a smiling adult male driver, seen through or reflected on a windshield, overlaid with an exterior view of a red vehicle. He appears to be seated behind the steering wheel, though the layered dissolve makes the view partly ambiguous.

More driving footage follows. A light-colored car is seen from the side on a road bordered by trees, again with a superimposed image of the driver’s face and interior reflections. The montage then shows a red sports car or coupe from a low, tilted angle, driving or cornering on a paved road with trees and hillside in the background.

The commercial ends on a black background with a large red “NISSAN” logo. Beneath it, in white italic lettering, is the phrase “MAJOR MOTION.” A small Nissan badge or logo appears centered near the bottom.
